channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Daniel Hall's Penalty Sends Auckland into Semi-finals
item.description: Auckland has secured a spot in the semi-finals thanks to a decisive penalty by Daniel Hall, who outperformed goalkeeper Patrick Beach. The match, which unfolded on May 2, 2026, saw Auckland progress a...

item.title: Nova Scotia Community Holds Rally on One-Year Anniversary of Missing Children
item.description: A Nova Scotia community is coming together to mark the first anniversary of the disappearance of siblings Lilly and Jack Sullivan, who went missing from their home in Lansdowne Station, Pictou County,...

item.title: Ugandan court sentences man to death for nursery school killings
item.description: In Kampala, Uganda, a court sentenced Christopher Okello Onyum to death for murdering four young children aged two and three at a local nursery school on April 2, 2026. The incident shocked the public...

item.title: West Bengal Police Imposes 60-Day Restrictions in Kolkata Ahead of Vote Counting
item.description: The West Bengal Police has imposed prohibitory orders for a period of 60 days, starting from Sunday, in selected areas of Kolkata’s central business district. This decision follows inputs received by ...

item.title: Cricket Australia abandons BBL franchise privatisation, explores alternative funding options
item.description: Cricket Australia (CA) has decided against selling partial or full ownership of its Big Bash League (BBL) franchises following objections from Queensland and New South Wales cricket authorities. The m...

item.title: NSA Ribadu’s Office Detains Nigerian Forest Security Service Chief and Six Others in Abuja
item.description: Dr. Osatimehin Joshua Wole, the Commander-General of the Nigerian Forest Security Service (NFSS), has been detained by the Office of the National Security Adviser (NSA), alongside six other members of...

item.title: Canada Explores Multiple Options for Energy Exports Amid Keystone XL Approval
item.description: Canada's Energy and Resource Development Minister, Tim Hodgson, addressed energy industry representatives in Calgary, emphasising the government's commitment to the country's energy sector. Highlighti...

item.title: US highlights lack of Zambian engagement as major health funding agreement remains unsigned
item.description: The United States has openly criticised the Zambian government for its lack of engagement regarding a new health aid memorandum of understanding that covers more than $1 billion in American funding. O...

item.title: Security Forces Neutralise 13 Terrorists in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa During Border Infiltration Attempts
item.description: Pakistan's security forces successfully foiled two separate infiltration attempts along the Pak-Afghan border, killing a total of 13 terrorists sponsored by India in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa. On April 28 an...

item.title: Growing interest in traditional hobbies reflects pushback against digital overload
item.description: An increasing number of people are rediscovering traditional, hands-on hobbies such as knitting, woodworking, gardening, and analogue crafts, and experts suggest this shift reflects a broader cultural...

item.title: Two Killed in Fatal Collision Between Bus and Truck on Calabar–Itu Highway
item.description: On Friday night around 9:25 PM, tragedy struck at Ikot Okon along the Calabar–Itu Highway when a coaster bus carrying passengers, reportedly students of Cross River State University, collided head-on ...

item.title: Kwara Governor AbdulRazaq Travels to France Amid Ongoing Insecurity in the State
item.description: Governor Abdulrahman AbdulRazaq of Kwara State don travel go France just as bandit attacks dey worsen for different communities inside the state. According to sources wey talk to SaharaReporters, the ...

item.title: China Railway Sets New Passenger Record of 24.8 Million On May 1st
item.description: On May 1st, 2026, China’s railway network set a new record by handling 24.8 million passengers in a single day, marking a significant increase in domestic travel for the May Day holiday. The China Sta...

item.title: A successful blind date in London leaves both participants keen to meet again
item.description: This instalment of the Guardian’s long-running Blind Date column follows Josh, a 30-year-old video game designer, and Hannah, a 31-year-old architectural lighting designer, as they meet for dinner in ...

item.title: Princess Charlotte celebrates 11th birthday with new portrait during Easter break
item.description: A new photograph of Princess Charlotte has been released to commemorate her 11th birthday. The image, captured by photographer Matt Porteous during the royal family's Easter holiday in Cornwall, shows...

item.title: Ottawa Weather: Mild Temperatures and Possible Showers Today
item.description: Ottawa is expected to experience a mild day on May 2, 2026, with temperatures remaining comfortable throughout the morning. Weather forecasts indicate a chance of showers later in the day, though no s...

item.title: Air Peace London–Lagos flight grounded temporarily after bird strike
item.description: Air Peace don clear wetin happen wey cause London (Gatwick) to Lagos flight on May 1, 2026, make e no fit continue. The airline yan say na bird strike cause am, wey na force majeure matter for aviatio...

item.title: Odisha Class 10 Board Results 2026: Access Information and Scorecard Download
item.description: The Odisha AHSC Class 10 Board Results for 2026 are officially released online. Students attempting to access their results via the NDTV education portal may face access restrictions due to server per...

item.title: Terrorists blast constable’s house with IED in Lakki Marwat, no one hurt
item.description: In a recent incident of terrorism in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, unknown militants planted an improvised explosive device at the house of Constable Tehsil Khan in the Shadikhel area of Lakki Marwat district. ...

item.title: KCB Bank Opens Multiple Job Opportunities Across Kenya, Uganda and Rwanda
item.description: Kenya Commercial Bank (KCB) Group has officially announced a range of job openings for professionals across Kenya, Uganda, and Rwanda. The vacancies cover entry-level, middle management, and senior ma...
